title: Variable Libraries for Environment-Specific Configuration
summary: Variable libraries are Microsoft's strategic Fabric capability for managing environment-specific settings across dev/test/prod. They store parameterized values (connection strings, paths, IDs) that items read at runtime, eliminating hardcoded environment references from item definitions.
when_to_use: Database connection strings that differ per environment
Storage account URLs (ADLS, Blob) per environment
Connection IDs that vary across workspaces
Item references that span workspace boundaries (multi-workspace solutions)
Any configuration value that changes between dev, test, and prod

variable_types.String: General-purpose text value (connection strings, URLs, paths)
variable_types.Connection reference: Reference to a Fabric connection — enables environment-specific data source binding
variable_types.Item reference: Reference to a Fabric item (workspace ID + item ID) — for cross-workspace dependencies

value_sets.concept: A value set provides alternate values for each variable. The variable library definition contains all value sets, but each workspace has a workspace-level setting for which set is active.
value_sets.naming_convention: Name value sets to match your deployment environment names (e.g., 'dev', 'test', 'prod'). This enables fabio deploy to auto-activate the matching set.
value_sets.default_set: The 'Default' value set is always present. It defines baseline values. Alternate sets only override variables that differ from default.
value_sets.activation: Only one value set can be active per workspace. Use 'fabio variable-library activate-value-set' or deploy with --env to auto-activate.

deploy_integration.ordering: Variable libraries are ALWAYS deployed first (before all other item types), ensuring values are available when dependent items are created.
deploy_integration.auto_activation: When deploying with --env <name>, fabio automatically activates the value set matching the environment name as a post-deploy hook.
workflow: 2. Edit variable library definition to add value sets for test/prod
4. fabio automatically activates the 'prod' value set after deploying the variable library

files: variables.json — {$schema, variables: [{name, type, value, note?}]} (all variables with defaults)
valueSets/<name>.json — {$schema, name, variableOverrides: [{name, value}]} (only overrides, NOT defaults)
definition_structure.git_representation: Each value set is a separate JSON file under valueSets/, enabling independent tracking of per-environment changes in source control.

multi_workspace_pattern.problem: A notebook in workspace A needs to write to a lakehouse in workspace B. The lakehouse ID differs per environment.
multi_workspace_pattern.solution: Use an Item reference variable in the variable library. The notebook reads the target lakehouse reference at runtime. Each environment's value set points to the correct lakehouse in that environment.
multi_workspace_pattern.benefit: Zero environment-specific code in the notebook definition. Cross-workspace dependencies are managed entirely through variable library value sets.

connection_reference_pattern.problem: Pipelines and shortcuts need connection IDs that differ per environment (different storage accounts).
connection_reference_pattern.solution: Use a Connection reference variable. The variable library stores the connection ID for each environment. ETL items read the connection at runtime.
connection_reference_pattern.benefit: Connection credentials stay in Fabric connections (managed by Terraform), not hardcoded in pipeline definitions.

agent_guidance.prefer_variable_libraries: For new Fabric solutions, prefer variable libraries over deploy parameter file find_replace rules for environment-specific configuration. Variable libraries are the Microsoft-recommended strategic approach.
agent_guidance.fallback_to_parameters: Use deploy parameters (find_replace, key_value_replace) only for item types that don't yet support reading from variable libraries, or for workspace-ID-level replacements.
agent_guidance.naming_alignment: Always name value sets identically to the --env values used with fabio deploy (e.g., 'dev', 'test', 'prod'). This enables auto-activation.
